text: Can water really remember? Science says no. In homeopathy, some claim water holds memory of substances. But chemistry tells a different story. Hydrogen bonds in water are fleeting, lasting only microseconds. And when diluted beyond Avogadro's number? There’s virtually no solute left. Just pure water. So, water’s memory? It’s a myth, not science. Like and follow for more science debunking!
